name: Montilla-Moriles Wine Route Circuit
name_original: Montilla y La Rambla desde Puente Genil — circular por Aguilar de la Frontera
description: This challenging 39.3-mile touring cycling route near Aguilar de la Frontera explores Andalusia's wine country with 2270 feet of elevation g
region_intro: Touring cycling around Puente Genil offers routes through expansive olive groves, significant natural wetlands, and historical greenways. The region is characterized by its agricultural landscapes, particularly along the Vía Verde del Aceite, and the presence of the Genil River. Cyclists can explore diverse ecosystems, from the protected Embalse de Cordobilla to the flat paths of the Vía Verde de la Campiña. The terrain generally features gentle gradients, making it suitable for various cycling abilities. Best touring cycling routes around Puente Genil The most popular touring cycling route is Cabra from Puente Genil — circular via the Vía Verde del Aceite, a 47.8 miles (76.9 km) trail that takes 4 hours 53 minutes to complete. This route follows a historic railway line through vast olive groves and features impressive viaducts. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Embalse de Cordobilla – Railway Bridge Singletrail loop from Puente Genil, a difficult 12.8 miles (20.6 km) path. This route explores the protected wetland area of Embalse de Cordobilla, known for its diverse birdlife and unique ecosystems. Local touring cyclists also love the Path next to the canal loop from Puente Genil, a 20.9 miles (33.6 km) trail leading through agricultural lands, often completed in about 2 hours 54 minutes. Touring cycling around Puente Genil is defined by extensive olive groves, historic greenways like the Vía Verde del Aceite, and protected wetlands such as Embalse de Cordobilla. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to more challenging long-distance routes. The routes in Puente Genil are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.75 stars from over 29 reviews. More than 283 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Puente Genil's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Puente Genil?
A: There are over 30 touring cycling routes around Puente Genil, offering a variety of experiences. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.75 stars from over 29 reviews.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while cycling in Puente Genil?
A: Touring cycling in Puente Genil takes you through diverse landscapes. You'll primarily encounter vast &lt;strong&gt;olive groves&lt;/strong&gt;, especially along the Vía Verde del Aceite. The region also features significant &lt;strong&gt;natural wetlands&lt;/strong&gt;, such as the Embalse de Cordobilla, and the scenic &lt;strong&gt;Genil River&lt;/strong&gt;. Expect a mix of agricultural lands and natural areas, often with gentle gradients.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Puente Genil?
A: Yes, Puente Genil offers routes suitable for various skill levels. While many routes are moderate or difficult, there are at least 2 easy touring cycling routes available. For a relatively easy option, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38172556" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Cordobilla loop from Puente Genil&lt;/a&gt;, which is 13.3 km long and explores the protected wetland area.

Q: What natural attractions can I explore by touring bike around Puente Genil?
A: You can explore several natural attractions. The &lt;strong&gt;Embalse de Cordobilla&lt;/strong&gt;, a protected wetland known for its birdlife, is accessible via routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24519437" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Cordobilla – Railway Bridge Singletrail loop from Puente Genil&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;strong&gt;Genil River Meander&lt;/strong&gt; is another notable natural feature you might encounter, which you can learn more about &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5392067" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;here&lt;/a&gt;. For more information on the natural sites, you can visit &lt;a href="https://visitpuentegenil.es/natural-site-of-cordobilla/" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;visitpuentegenil.es&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes near Puente Genil?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025882643/cabra-from-puente-genil-circular-via-the-via-verde-del-aceite" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabra from Puente Genil — circular via the Vía Verde del Aceite&lt;/a&gt;, a 76.9 km trail following a historic railway line. Another circular route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40317122" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Path next to the canal loop from Puente Genil&lt;/a&gt;, which is 33.6 km long and leads through agricultural lands.

Q: Can I cycle along the Vía Verde del Aceite from Puente Genil?
A: Absolutely. The Vía Verde del Aceite is one of Andalusia's most significant greenways and is easily accessible from Puente Genil. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025882643/cabra-from-puente-genil-circular-via-the-via-verde-del-aceite" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabra from Puente Genil — circular via the Vía Verde del Aceite&lt;/a&gt; utilize this historic railway line, offering smooth paths through olive groves and over impressive 19th-century metal viaducts.

Q: What historical or cultural landmarks can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: While cycling, you can encounter several historical points of interest. The Vía Verde del Aceite itself is a historical route, featuring old railway infrastructure like viaducts. You might also pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4737340" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Navas del Selpillar station&lt;/a&gt;. In Puente Genil itself, historical landmarks include the Puente de la Unión and the La Alianza Flour Mill.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Puente Genil?
A: The region's climate makes spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) ideal for touring cycling. During these seasons, temperatures are generally mild and pleasant, making long rides more comfortable. Summers can be very hot, and winters can be cooler and wetter, though still often suitable for cycling on sunny days.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of vineyards or wine regions?
A: Yes, the region is known for its wine production. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027265261/montilla-moriles-wine-route-circuit" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montilla-Moriles Wine Route Circuit&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through the renowned Montilla-Moriles wine region. You might also spot the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/893911" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montilla Vineyards&lt;/a&gt; as a highlight in the area.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of touring cycling routes in Puente Genil?
A: The touring cycling routes around Puente Genil offer a range of difficulties. Out of 33 routes, 2 are classified as easy, 18 as moderate, and 13 as difficult. This variety ensures options for cyclists of all experience levels, from leisurely rides to more challenging long-distance tours.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints or scenic spots along the touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, the routes offer several scenic spots. Along the Vía Verde del Aceite, you'll find panoramic views of expansive olive groves and impressive 19th-century metal viaducts. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5394288" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Malpasillo Dam and Genil River&lt;/a&gt; highlight is another point of interest offering scenic vistas.

Q: Can I find routes that pass by significant natural wetlands?
A: Absolutely. The region is home to important natural wetlands. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24519437" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Cordobilla – Railway Bridge Singletrail loop from Puente Genil&lt;/a&gt; is a route that specifically explores the protected wetland area of Embalse de Cordobilla, known for its diverse birdlife and unique ecosystems. The Laguna de Zóñar Nature Reserve is also a vital wetland in the area.
tips: Between La Rambla and Puente Genil on the A-3133 there are endless olive and almond plantations.
Town that belongs to the Montilla-Moriles Wine Route. Being agriculture its axis of economic activity thanks to the fertility of its lands, among its main crops are garlic, onion, olive and melon.

La Rambla is a Spanish city in the province of Córdoba with around 7,500 inhabitants.

The main source of wealth is agriculture, although the emerging and thriving pottery industry stands out, with more than 110 factories distributing their products throughout the national market and gradually gaining a foothold in the international market as well (translated from Wikipedia).

Laguna de Zóñar is a "Wetland of International Importance". It belongs to the Inventory of Protected Natural Areas of the Junta de Andalucía. It is the only natural lake in Andalusia, reaching a depth of 14 meters. It is the habitat of more than 30 species of birds, which we can observe walking through this unique place.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Montilla y La Rambla desde Puente Genil cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as a difficult touring bicycle ride. It is a 63.3-kilometer circular route with a significant elevation gain of 690 meters, indicating a hilly landscape. It requires a very good fitness level to complete, and some sections might be rougher or unpaved, potentially requiring cyclists to push their bikes.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is predominantly on paved surfaces, but some sections may be rougher or unpaved. The route features a hilly landscape, winding through ancient vineyards and olive groves typical of the Montilla-Moriles wine region.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The estimated duration for this touring bicycle route is approximately 3 hours and 56 minutes, though this can vary based on individual fitness and stops along the way.

faqs.question: What can I see along the Montilla y La Rambla desde Puente Genil cycling route?
faqs.answer: You can expect picturesque views of the Andalusian countryside, with vast expanses of vineyards and olive groves. The route offers immersion in the Montilla-Moriles Wine Route and showcases architectural gems such as churches, castles, and hermitages. Specific highlights include Olive and almond fields, Montalbán de Córdoba, and the Laguna de Zóñar Visitor Center.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this cycling route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas are not detailed, as a circular route starting from Puente Genil, you would typically look for parking within Puente Genil or a nearby town like Aguilar de la Frontera to begin your ride. Public transport options to these towns may also be available.

faqs.question: Is the Montilla y La Rambla desde Puente Genil cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Komoot routes are generally open to dogs, but given this is a touring bicycle route, it's important to consider your dog's endurance and safety if they are running alongside. Always ensure your dog is well-behaved and on a leash, especially when passing through towns or near traffic.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this trail?
faqs.answer: No specific permits or entrance fees are required to cycle this route, as it primarily uses public roads and trails through the Andalusian countryside.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Montilla-Moriles region experiences a Mediterranean climate. Spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for cycling, avoiding the intense heat of summer.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Camino del Término - Era del Mirador, Camino Vecinal, Camino de La Rambla, Camino del Cuadrado, Vereda del Cambrón, Paraje Cerro del Humo, Camino de la Zarza, Camino Las Moreras, and Camino del Carreron.

faqs.question: Are there places to find food or drink along the route?
faqs.answer: The route traverses the Montilla-Moriles wine region and passes through towns like Montalbán de Córdoba. You can expect to find cafes, restaurants, and shops in these settlements to refuel and hydrate.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail or out-and-back?
faqs.answer: This is a circular (loop) route, meaning it starts and ends in the same general area, allowing you to return to your starting point without retracing your path.
